01_initial_request.input_data: Native retinas from homozygous P23H knock-in mice (Rho^{P23H/P23H}) and wild-type mice (Rho^{+/+})
01_initial_request.user_intent: Identify proteins that photoreceptors employ to recognize and target misfolded rhodopsin for degradation to advance therapeutic potential for Retinitis Pigmentosa
01_initial_request.quantifiable_goal: Identification of 200+ P23H rhodopsin protein binding partners via mass spectrometry and comparative interactome analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] P23H rhodopsin is a misfolded variant causing retinitis pigmentosa, but the mechanisms by which photoreceptors recognize and target it for degradation are incompletely understood. [Gap] Need to obtain native retinal tissue from appropriate mouse models to study P23H rhodopsin interactome. [Decision] To achieve protein interactome identification, chose to use homozygous P23H knock-in mice (Rho^{P23H/P23H}) and wild-type mice (Rho^{+/+}) at postnatal day 15 to perform retina harvesting on target, expecting to obtain viable photoreceptor-containing retinal tissue.

parameters.mouse_strains: Rho^{P23H/P23H} and Rho^{+/+} in C57BL/J6 background

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Retinas collected from 24 Rho^{+/+} mice and 40 Rho^{P23H/P23H} mice at P15 when viable photoreceptors are still present

references.citation: Sakami, S. et al. J. Biol. Chem. 286, 10551–10567 (2011)
references.relevance: Describes the P23H knock-in mouse model used in this study

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Retinal tissue has been harvested from mouse models. [Gap] Need to extract proteins from retinas while preserving protein interactions for immunoprecipitation. [Decision] To achieve protein extraction, chose to use lysis buffer containing n-Dodecyl-β-D-maltoside to perform solubilization on retinal tissue, expecting to obtain retinal protein lysates.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Retinal protein lysates generated from pooled retinas (24 Rho^{+/+} or 40 Rho^{P23H/P23H} mice)

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Retinal protein lysates have been prepared. [Gap] Need to specifically isolate rhodopsin protein complexes from lysates. [Decision] To achieve rhodopsin immunopurification, chose to use 1D4 anti-rhodopsin antibody with Dynabeads Protein G to perform immunoprecipitation on protein lysates, expecting to obtain rhodopsin protein complexes.

parameters.elution_buffer: PBS containing 8 M urea and 0.5% n-dodecyl-β-D-maltoside at 80°C

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Rhodopsin immunoprecipitated from Rho^{+/+}, Rho^{P23H/P23H}, and MEF lysates

references.relevance: 1D4 antibody recognizes the 9-amino acid carboxyl termini of rhodopsin

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Rhodopsin has been immunoprecipitated from retinal lysates. [Gap] Need to confirm successful recovery of rhodopsin protein before proceeding to mass spectrometry. [Decision] To achieve validation of immunoprecipitation, chose to use 1D4 anti-rhodopsin antibody with HRP-coupled secondary antibody to perform immunoblotting on eluates, expecting to confirm abundant recovery of wild-type and P23H rhodopsin protein.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Immunoblotting confirmed abundant recovery of wild-type and P23H rhodopsin protein in immunoprecipitation eluates

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Rhodopsin immunoprecipitation has been validated. [Gap] Need to identify protein interaction partners of rhodopsin through proteomic analysis. [Decision] To achieve protein identification, chose to use LTQ-Orbitrap mass spectrometer to perform LC-MS/MS on immunopurified rhodopsin eluates, expecting to identify rhodopsin-interacting proteins.

parameters.digestion: Sequencing Grade Modified Trypsin overnight at 37°C
parameters.analysis_pipeline: Integrated Proteomics Pipeline (IP2) with ProLuCID and DTASelect2

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Identified 276 proteins in wild-type rhodopsin LC-MS/MS and 286 proteins in P23H rhodopsin LC-MS/MS

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] LC-MS/MS has been performed on rhodopsin immunoprecipitates. [Gap] Need to assess specificity of rhodopsin-interacting proteins by comparing to negative control. [Decision] To achieve specificity validation, chose to use LC-MS/MS to perform analysis on MEF protein lysate eluates immunopurified with 1D4, expecting to confirm absence of rhodopsin and rhodopsin-interacting proteins in control.

parameters.analysis_pipeline: Integrated Proteomics Pipeline (IP2) with ProLuCID and DTASelect2

02_agent_trajectory.observation: MEF LC-MS/MS showed no rhodopsin protein fragments and vast majority of wild-type and P23H rhodopsin interactome proteins were absent

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Mass spectrometry has identified protein interaction partners. [Gap] Need to determine functional pathways and biological processes enriched in P23H vs wild-type rhodopsin interactomes. [Decision] To achieve functional annotation, chose to use gProfiler to perform pathway enrichment analysis on protein lists, expecting to identify significant GO, Reactome, and KEGG terms.

parameters.databases: Gene Ontology (GO-BP, GO-MF, GO-CC), Reactome, KEGG
parameters.input: P23H and wild-type rhodopsin protein lists from mass spectrometry

02_agent_trajectory.observation: GO-BP analysis revealed P23H interactome enriched in translation, ER protein folding, ER stress, and ERAD terms; wild-type enriched in phototransduction and protein folding

references.citation: Raudvere, U. et al. Nucleic Acids Res. 47, W191–W198 (2019)
references.relevance: gProfiler web-based application for functional enrichment analysis

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Pathway enrichment analysis has been completed using gProfiler. [Gap] Need to visualize and cluster enriched terms to identify common cellular themes. [Decision] To achieve network visualization, chose to use Cytoscape with Enrichment Map plugin to perform enrichment map visualization on gProfiler results, expecting to organize terms into clusters.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Enrichment map visualization created showing significant terms from all databases

references.citation: Merico, D. et al. PLoS ONE 5, e13984 (2010)
references.relevance: Enrichment Map plugin for gene-set enrichment visualization

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Enrichment map has been created in Cytoscape. [Gap] Need to group similar terms into functional clusters. [Decision] To achieve term clustering, chose to use clusterMaker2 plugin to perform clustering on enrichment map nodes, expecting to group terms by similarity of overlapping genes.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Terms grouped into clusters based on similarity

references.relevance: clusterMaker2 for network clustering in Cytoscape

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Terms have been clustered in Cytoscape. [Gap] Need to automatically label clusters with semantic annotations. [Decision] To achieve cluster annotation, chose to use Auto Annotate plugin to perform cluster labeling on clustered terms, expecting to label clusters by word frequencies.

parameters.labeling_method: dependent on word frequencies of the terms

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Clusters labeled with key themes: eukaryotic translation, ER stress, ubiquitination, synapses for P23H; photodetection/phototransduction and G-protein binding shared with wild-type

references.relevance: AutoAnnotate app for summarizing networks with semantic annotations

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Pathway enrichment and clustering analysis completed. [Gap] Need to identify protein-protein interaction networks within the P23H rhodopsin interactome. [Decision] To achieve PPI network construction, chose to use STRING database to perform protein-protein interaction network assembly on P23H rhodopsin interactome proteins, expecting to identify interacting protein networks.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: Found 741 interactions between the 173 proteins unique to P23H rhodopsin

references.citation: Szklarczyk, D. et al. Nucleic Acids Res. 47, D607–D613 (2019)
references.relevance: STRING database for protein-protein interaction networks

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PPI network has been constructed using STRING. [Gap] Need to identify highly interconnected protein modules within the network. [Decision] To achieve module identification, chose to use MCODE plugin to perform module detection on STRING PPI network, expecting to identify significant protein interaction modules.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: One PPI module identified at high stringency (MCODE value = 21) composed of 20 highly interconnected proteins including 15 ribosome subunit components

references.citation: Bader, G.D. & Hogue, C.W. BMC Bioinformatics 4, 1 (2003)
references.relevance: MCODE for molecular complex detection in networks

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] PPI modules have been identified. [Gap] Need to identify the most interconnected hub proteins within the network. [Decision] To achieve hub gene identification, chose to use cytoHubba plugin to perform connectivity ranking on PPI network, expecting to identify top 10 most interconnected proteins.
02_agent_trajectory.observation: 10 hub genes identified: 7 from ribosome-related PPI module (Ubb, Uba52, Rps6, Eef1a2, Plec, Rps8, Rpl19) plus Actb, DHX9, and Rack1

references.citation: Chin, C.H. et al. BMC Syst Biol 8, S11 (2014)
references.relevance: cytoHubba for hub gene discovery in Cytoscape

02_agent_trajectory.thought: [Background] Hub genes and PPI modules have been identified. [Gap] Need to determine subcellular localization patterns of P23H vs wild-type rhodopsin interacting proteins. [Decision] To achieve cellular compartment analysis, chose to use Gene Ontology Cellular Compartment (GO-CC) database to perform subcellular distribution analysis on interactome proteins, expecting to identify mislocalization patterns.

parameters.comparison: P23H vs wild-type rhodopsin interacting proteins

02_agent_trajectory.observation: Marked increase of P23H rhodopsin interacting proteins in synapse, cytoskeleton, axon, and ribosome compartments compared to wild-type

references.citation: Gene Ontology Consortium. Nucleic Acids Res. 49, D325–D334 (2021)
references.relevance: GO-CC database for subcellular localization annotation

03_success_verification.validation_technique: Comparative mass spectrometry with pathway enrichment and protein-protein interaction network analysis

P23H_proteins_identified.interpretation: Total proteins associated with P23H rhodopsin identified by LC-MS/MS

wildtype_proteins_identified.interpretation: Total proteins associated with wild-type rhodopsin identified by LC-MS/MS

shared_proteins.interpretation: Proteins common between wild-type and P23H rhodopsin interactomes

P23H_unique_proteins.interpretation: Proteins unique to P23H rhodopsin interactome

wildtype_unique_proteins.interpretation: Proteins unique to wild-type rhodopsin interactome

PPI_module_proteins.interpretation: Proteins in the most significant PPI module (MCODE value = 21)

hub_genes_identified.interpretation: Top 10 most interconnected proteins in P23H rhodopsin interactome network

03_success_verification.final_verdict: Research objective achieved: Successfully identified 286 P23H rhodopsin protein binding partners (exceeding the 200+ goal) via mass spectrometry and comparative interactome analysis. The study revealed key quality control mechanisms including ERAD, ribosome quality control (RQC), and translational quality control pathways that photoreceptors employ to recognize and target misfolded P23H rhodopsin for degradation, providing novel therapeutic targets for Retinitis Pigmentosa.
